digest.top_line: ROADS: resected brain mets >2cm — surg-bed recurrence 1% GammaTile brachytherapy vs 12% post-op SRS (randomized, N=230), reopening cavity management.
digest.tldr: RT modality and sequencing carried the day: ROADS makes cavity brachytherapy a randomized contender against post-op SRS, and Neo-CRAG revives a neoadjuvant chemoRT survival benefit TOPGEAR missed (mOS 67.5 vs 37.6mo). Prostate ran deep on biomarker-gated intensification (TALAPRO-3, Decipher), but the RT-actionable signals sat in CNS and upper-GI.

sites.intro: Biomarker-gated intensification dominated: Decipher and clinico-transcriptomic scores gate systemic add-ons on an RT+ADT backbone, but most signals are prognostic, not predictive.

studies.name: A-DREAM (ALLIANCE mAPMS)
studies.tldr: 41% (32/78) remained treatment-free with eugonadal testosterone at 18mo after interrupting ADT+ARPI in deep-responding mHSPC; primary EP met.
details: 🔍 Single-arm phase 2 (ALLIANCE A-DREAM); N=78 eligible mHSPC, enrolled 07/2022-03/2024. No randomised continuous-therapy comparator.
🔍 Deep-responder selection: PSA<0.2 (stable/falling) after 18-24mo ADT + ≥12mo ARPI; 64.9% low-volume (CHAARTED).
🔍 Re-initiation triggers: PSA ≥5 ng/mL, radiographic change (PCWG3), or PrCa-related symptoms.
🔍 29.5% received radiation to metastatic sites pre-interruption; 51.3% had prior radiation as local therapy.
⚠️ 4 of 29 who resumed ADT+ARPI per protocol later progressed (2 rPD, 1 PDu, 1 PET) and discontinued original ARPI.
⚠️ Lenient success bar: primary EP read on 80% CI + one-sided p; ~59% did not achieve treatment-free + T-recovery at 18mo.
⚠️ Short FU (26.9mo); OS (4/78 events) and rPFS median NE — durability of the off-treatment interval still unknown.
🔗 Intermittent-therapy concept echoes SWOG 9346 (intermittent ADT, mHSPC), but here a deeper-response, post-ARPI-intensification population with both agents interrupted.

verdict.rationale: Single-arm phase 2, no randomised comparator vs continuous ADT+ARPI; primary EP judged on 80% CI / one-sided p; rPFS and OS immature (median NE at 26.9mo).
verdict.audience: mHSPC, deep responders: PSA<0.2 after 18-24mo ADT + ≥12mo ARPI

open_questions: Does interruption compromise long-term OS vs continuous ADT+ARPI?
Biomarkers predicting which deep responders can safely interrupt
Durability of the treatment-free interval beyond 2 years

studies.name: ARACOG (AFT-47)
studies.tldr: Enzalutamide drove greater 24-wk cognitive decline than darolutamide (MCCD -36.1 vs -15.8, p=0.009) in advanced prostate cancer.
details: 🔍 Randomized open-label phase 2 (Alliance, AFT-47), N=111 advanced prostate cancer spanning mHSPC, mCRPC, nmCRPC.
🔍 1° EP: Maximally Changed Cognitive Domain (MCCD), % change baseline→24wk across 5 remote CANTAB tests (executive function, visual memory, attention, working memory).
🔍 Stratified by age (<65 / 65-80 / >80); darolutamide supplied by study, enzalutamide via standard of care; enrolled 8/2021 to 3/2025.
⚠️ MCCD picks each arm's worst-hit domain, so the headline numbers reflect different domains (daro PALFAM, enz SWM), not a like-for-like comparison.
⚠️ Open-label design; CANTAB is computerized/objective, but expectancy effects on test effort aren't excluded.
⚠️ Cognitive surrogate: CANTAB modules are research tools, not for clinical diagnosis (per slide); link to functional decline unestablished.
⚠️ 24-wk analysis: daro n=48, enz n=47 (of 111 randomized); crossover pts scored at crossover within their randomized arm.
🔗 Direction aligns with darolutamide's low blood-brain-barrier penetration vs enzalutamide (known pharmacology), giving the divergence a mechanistic basis.

verdict.rationale: Randomized open-label phase 2 head-to-head; prespecified MCCD endpoint hit. Cognitive surrogate, small N, open-label temper it. Consistent with daro's low CNS penetration.
verdict.audience: Advanced prostate cancer on ARSI (mHSPC, mCRPC, or nmCRPC)

open_questions: Durability of cognitive divergence beyond 24 weeks
Confirmation in a larger blinded head-to-head
Whether MCCD differences translate to functional cognitive outcomes

studies.name: NRG Clinico-Transcriptomic Risk Stratification
studies.tldr: ~¼ of NCCN high-risk pts reclassified when 22-gene GC added to clinical risk; combined score gates RT+ADT vs RT+ADT+AAP intensification.
details: 🔍 Clinico-transcriptomic system layers a 22-gene GC (Decipher) on NCCN risk in ≥high-risk localized prostate; RT+ADT is the uniform backbone
📊 22-gene GC independently prognostic for MFS, DM, and OS beyond clinical variables alone (p<0.001)
📊 ~¼ of the ≥high-risk population has discordant clinical vs biomarker risk

details.text: 🔍 Combined score gates systemic intensification on the RT+ADT backbone

details: 📊 STAMPEDE AAP absolute benefit grows over time: up to ~20% MFS, ~13% OS (Attard, Lancet 2022)
⚠️ GC shown prognostic, not predictive: per-stratum AAP benefit is inferred from STAMPEDE, not randomized within GC-defined groups
⚠️ Retrospective biomarker model; CT classification not prospectively validated for the intensification decision itself
🔗 Extends prior VHR framework (Spratt et al, JCO 2018) to the modern very-high-risk population

verdict.rationale: Retrospective biomarker model; 22-gene GC shown prognostic, not predictive. Intensification rule infers per-stratum AAP benefit from STAMPEDE, not randomized within GC-defined groups.
verdict.audience: NCCN high-risk/very-high-risk localized prostate, RT+ADT candidates

studies.significance: The actionable question is systemic intensification on a fixed RT+ADT backbone: layering the 22-gene GC (Decipher) on NCCN clinical risk reclassifies ~¼ of high-risk pts, so clinical staging alone misassigns who gets abiraterone. GC is independently prognostic for MFS, DM, and OS (p<0.001).
open_questions: Does GC-high risk predict abiraterone benefit, not just prognosis?
Prospective validation of the clinico-genomic intensification thresholds
Can GC-low very-high-risk pts safely omit abiraterone?

studies.name: GU Biomarkers in Clinical Practice
studies.tldr: Decipher/clinical-risk discordant in 24% of events; genomic classifiers validated pre-ARPI may not transfer to current ARPI-based SOC.
details: 🔬 Theme: applying genomic biomarkers to therapy selection in advanced prostate; only as good as the validation population

details.text: 💊 Biomarker-gated therapy fractions, advanced prostate (2026 landscape)

details: 💊 Tissue panel testing for HRR + tumor-suppressor loss is SOC; 'genomics is not genetics'
⚠️ Decipher vs clinical risk discordant in 24% of patient events
⚠️ Tissue-availability bias: biomarker cohort n=427 vs full trial n=3816

subdetails: Final biomarker cohort N=634 (56% of enrolled)

details: 🔍 Docetaxel not randomized; protocol amended after 88 pts to allow it per CHARTED
📊 Decipher ≤0.85 subgroup: ADT+enza+docetaxel had worse OS than ADT+enza
⚠️ Docetaxel subgroup confounded: more high-volume disease + older age → selection bias

figures.caption: Discordance in 24% of events; biomarker tissue n=427 vs full cohort n=3816.

studies.significance: The portability caveat is the read for anyone using Decipher: it disagreed with clinical risk in 24% of events and was derived in pre-ARPI, tissue-selected subsets (n=427 of 3816). Weigh that before trusting a genomic classifier to gate intensification in current ARPI-based practice.
open_questions: Do genomic classifiers validated pre-ARPI transfer to current ARPI-based SOC?
Optimal genomic biomarker for systemic intensification in mHSPC
Does the Decipher ≤0.85 docetaxel-harm signal survive selection-bias adjustment?

studies.name: ENZAMET + Decipher
studies.tldr: Docetaxel-by-Decipher interaction p=0.04: triplet helps high-Decipher (>0.85) pts (OS HR 0.75) but not low-Decipher (≤0.85, HR 1.94) in mHSPC.
details: 🔍 Post-hoc Decipher biomarker analysis of ENZAMET; ADT+ENZA+docetaxel cohort N=320, DPMC cutoff 0.85
🔍 Docetaxel investigator-allocated within ENZAMET, not randomized; compared by propensity-score (IPTW) weighted MVA
📊 Docetaxel benefit confined to high-Decipher (>0.85); low-Decipher pts show none, supporting docetaxel omission
🔗 Decipher >0.85 prognostic for worse OS on ADT+ENZA alone (HR 3.02, 1.50-5.76)
📐 MVA covariates: tumor volume, Gleason, synchronous vs metachronous, ECOG, age
⚠️ Within-group docetaxel HRs non-significant (CIs cross 1); read rests on the interaction test, not a positive arm
⚠️ Residual imbalance after propensity scoring per authors; observational, non-randomized docetaxel comparison
🔗 Framed Level 1B, consistent with CHAARTED and STAMPEDE docetaxel data

verdict.rationale: Post-hoc biomarker subgroup of ENZAMET; docetaxel investigator-allocated not randomized, residual confounding after IPTW, within-group HRs non-significant. Signal rests on interaction test only.

open_questions: Prospective validation of Decipher-guided docetaxel intensification in mHSPC
Whether docetaxel can be safely omitted in low-Decipher pts

studies.name: TALAPRO-3
studies.tldr: 3-yr rPFS 77% vs 56%, HR 0.48 (95% CI 0.36-0.65), p<0.001, adding talazoparib to enza+ADT in HRR-deficient mHSPC.
details: 📊 ITT median rPFS NC vs 45.8mo (37.7-NC); 67/300 vs 126/299 events
🔍 Phase 3 RCT: talazoparib+enza vs placebo+enza, both with ADT; 1L metastatic HRR-deficient prostate cancer; N=599 ITT
🔍 Effect gradient by genotype: deepest in BRCA, attenuated but still positive in non-BRCA HRR (CI excludes 1)
⚠️ rPFS is the surrogate 1° EP; OS not reported in source, follow-up immature (tala median NC in every subgroup)
🔗 vs TALAPRO-2 (1L mCRPC): same talazoparib+enza doublet, now moved earlier into HRR-deficient disease
⚠️ Added PARP toxicity (anemia, fatigue) not reported in source; gates the risk/benefit in an earlier, less-symptomatic population

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase 3, biomarker-selected, 1° rPFS hit decisively (HR 0.48) in a setting where the PARP+ARSI doublet isn't yet standard; OS still immature.

open_questions: OS benefit beyond rPFS surrogate

sites.intro: Bladder-preservation chemoRT (55Gy/20fx) is the throughline: durvalumab adds to trimodality (RAD-IO) while EV+pembro threatens to replace it (EV309 vs chemoRT).

studies.name: RAD-IO
studies.tldr: 12-mo DFS 40/50 (80%) cleared the GO threshold for durvalumab added to 5FU/MMC chemoRT in muscle-invasive bladder, bladder-preservation intent.
details: 📊 1° outcome: 12-mo DFS 40/50 (80%), 95% CI 0.67-0.89, cleared pre-set GO threshold (≥75%) for further evaluation
🔍 Single-arm multi-stage feasibility/safety trial, N=55 (54 treated), MIBC, definitive bladder-preservation intent (Abstract 4504, ND James)
🔍 RT: 55Gy/20fx to bladder; node-positive pts add 46Gy/20fx elective nodal RT (Stage 1 expansion, first 6 node+ pts)
💊 ChemoRT backbone: concurrent 5-FU + mitomycin C (BC2001 regimen)
💊 Durvalumab neoadjuvant, synchronous with chemoRT, then adjuvant for 12 months
⚠️ Single-arm, benchmarked vs historical BC2001; no contemporaneous chemoRT-alone or durvalumab-omission arm, so the IO contribution can't be isolated
⚠️ 12-mo DFS is an early surrogate; bladder-intact survival, OS, and late GU/GI toxicity not reported in source
🔗 Contextual benchmark BC2001 (JCO 2016), DFS by chemo randomisation: HR 0.78 (0.60-1.02), p=0.07

verdict.rationale: Single-arm feasibility trial, no randomised chemoRT-alone or durvalumab comparator; benchmarked against historical BC2001; 12-mo DFS surrogate at an early timepoint.
verdict.audience: Muscle-invasive bladder cancer, cT2-T3, 13% node+, 75% prior neoadjuvant chemo

studies.significance: RT-relevant extension is nodal coverage: node-positive pts (n=7) received 46Gy/20fx elective nodal RT plus 55Gy/20fx to bladder, pushing chemoRT bladder preservation past its usual node-negative limit. Dose is the hypofractionated BC2001 UK schedule (55Gy/20fx), not conventional fractionation, which gates transferability to US practice. Moves whether to add adjuvant IO and extend preservation to node+ MIBC.
open_questions: Randomised durvalumab vs chemoRT-alone comparison needed
Durability of bladder-intact survival and DFS beyond 12 months
Bladder-preservation outcomes in node-positive MIBC

studies.tldr: Discussant roundup balancing cure vs function: durvalumab+chemoRT kept full bladder RT delivery; EV+pembro bladder-sparing (EV309 vs chemoRT) and ctDNA RCC selection maturing.
details: 🔍 ASCO 2026 GU discussant summary (Brian Rini): curative-intent bladder + RCC, framed as improving cure while preserving function/QoL
📊 Bladder preservation arm: durvalumab added to 55 Gy/20 fx chemoRT (mitomycin C + 5-FU backbone)
📊 Per Rini: durvalumab didn't compromise RT delivery, and chemo delivery not significantly compromised
⚠️ Deliverability-only readout (N=54); long-term bladder preservation, function, symptom burden, safety and QoL still require follow-up (Rini)
⚠️ EV309 randomizes EV+pembrolizumab against chemoradiotherapy, directly pitting systemic bladder-sparing against trimodality
⚠️ RAMPART non-clear cell RCC subgroups: few pts/events, wide CIs, point estimates unstable; central pathology review ongoing
⚠️ Adjuvant IO has no proven benefit in non-clear cell RCC (per Rini)
🔗 Adjuvant RCC ctDNA (Choueiri, ASCO 2026): ctDNA status refines selection beyond pathologic features; ctDNA-positive pts had substantially shorter DFS
studies.significance: The radonc read is bladder preservation under competition: durvalumab added to 55Gy/20fx MMC/5-FU chemoRT kept full RT delivery in 54/54 pts (87% without delay), but EV309 now randomizes EV+pembro against chemoradiotherapy itself. If systemic bladder-sparing matures, trimodality's role narrows.

studies.name: PREPEC
studies.tldr: Pre-pectoral implant improved 24mo chest physical well-being (BREAST-Q +4.8, p=0.01) but failed non-inferiority on implant loss.
details: 🔍 International multicenter RCT: pre- vs sub-pectoral implant plane after skin/nipple-sparing mastectomy
🔍 Population: therapeutic (cancer) and risk-reduction (prophylactic) mastectomy pts
🔍 1° EP: BREAST-Q chest physical well-being at 24mo, 0-100 scale (higher = better)
⚠️ Safety non-inferiority NOT met: more unplanned implant loss/replacement with pre-pectoral, CI upper bound past the NI margin
⚠️ Open-label (surgical plane can't be blinded); subjective PRO endpoint vulnerable to expectation bias
⚠️ 24-mo f/u short for implant durability, late loss/capsular contracture can accrue beyond 2yr
📐 Longitudinal completion 83-95% across 6 timepoints; multiple-imputation pooled LS means
🔗 Randomized evidence on implant plane; pre-pectoral uptake had largely rested on retrospective series

verdict.rationale: Randomized, 1° PRO endpoint met, aligns with field's shift to pre-pectoral; safety non-inferiority unmet but implant-loss CI crosses zero. Tradeoff bars practice-changing.
verdict.audience: Skin/nipple-sparing mastectomy, implant reconstruction; cancer or prophylactic

details.text: 📊 Efficacy by arm (BICR), asandeutertinib vs osimertinib

details: ⚠️ Surrogate endpoints only (iORR, PFS); no OS reported in source
⚠️ Overall PFS edge is marginal, p=0.0473 just clears significance; the intracranial signal is the stronger result
⚠️ Phase II, both PFS medians not reached in experimental arm → follow-up still immature

⚠️ Source is a branded social-media infographic of an LBA, not peer-reviewed primary data

details: 🔍 Phase 3, randomized 1:1, open-label, multicenter; N=413 (208 vs 205)

💊 Sac-TMT (TROP2 ADC) 4 mg/kg Q2W + pembro 400 mg Q6W vs pembro mono; pembro max 18 cycles

⚠️ Open-label, but primary PFS by blinded independent central review (BICR) limits assessment bias
⚠️ Comparator is pembro mono; in PD-L1 TPS 1-49% chemo-IO is standard, so benefit partly vs weaker comparator
⚠️ No safety/AE data in source tweets; adding a TROP2 ADC carries toxicity beyond pembro alone
🔗 1L TROP2-ADC+IO data in NSCLC; single-agent TROP2 ADCs (e.g. dato-DXd) showed modest activity in pretreated disease

verdict.rationale: Randomised phase 3 beats pembro mono on PFS (BICR), contesting 1L SOC; but OS immature, open-label, and pembro-mono comparator weak in PD-L1 1-49%.

🔍 Randomized, N=230, resected brain mets >2 cm: intraoperative GammaTile brachytherapy vs post-op SRS (ASCO 2026 final, Weinberg)
⚠️ Open-label by design (brachy vs SRS unblindable); surg-bed-recurrence adjudication could be biased
⚠️ 2-yr OS gap (62% vs 36%) large for a purely local therapy; query selection imbalance or systemic-disease confounding
🔗 Post-op cavity SRS is current standard (N107C/CEC.3); no prior randomized data established brachytherapy over SRS

verdict.rationale: Randomized vs current standard (post-op SRS) with primary surg-bed-control endpoint met, but open-label and an OS magnitude implausibly large for local therapy keep it short of practice-changing.

studies.significance: Both arms are RT, so the actionable choice is GammaTile intraop brachytherapy vs post-op SRS for a resected brain met >2cm. The surg-bed-control win is large, but leptomeningeal failure ran 10% with GammaTile vs 3% with SRS, a brachytherapy pattern-of-failure cost that should temper adoption despite the headline OS edge.
open_questions: Durability of OS benefit with longer follow-up
Whether the OS difference reflects local control or selection imbalance
Leptomeningeal disease risk with GammaTile vs SRS

sites.intro: Celiac plexus SRS for cancer pain, with a SPIN score (severe baseline pain + intact nerves) stratifying response 32→89%.

studies.name: SPIN Score / Celiac Plexus SRS
studies.tldr: Pain response 32→53→89% across SPIN score 0/1/2 after celiac SRS; severe baseline pain + no prior neurotoxic chemo predict response.
details: 🔍 Post-hoc analysis of 90 evaluable pts from the phase 2 trial (NCT03323489, Lancet Oncol 2024)
📊 Parent-trial pain response 53% (95% CI 42-64%); ≥2-pt BPI-SF drop at 3 weeks
🔍 SPIN (Severe Pain + Intact Nerves): baseline pain >6 (+1), no prior neurotoxic chemo (+1)

details.text: 📐 Independent predictors on multivariate logistic regression

details: 📊 Response climbs with baseline pain severity; >6 cutoff chosen for optimal discrimination
⚠️ Age and therapy line significant on univariate but lost to collinearity in multivariate
📐 2-variable model AUC 0.716 apparent, 0.714 optimism-corrected (500-iteration bootstrap)
⚠️ Post-hoc, internally derived score; n=19 in the top group; external validation not yet done

verdict.rationale: Post-hoc predictor analysis of a single-arm phase 2 trial; internally derived score, optimism-corrected AUC 0.71, external validation pending.
verdict.audience: Pancreatic cancer with intractable retroperitoneal pain

studies.significance: The actionable read is timing: pts with severe baseline pain and no prior neurotoxic chemo (the score's 'intact nerves' arm) reached 89% pain response vs 32% with neither, arguing for celiac SRS referral before chemo neuropathy sets in. Neurotoxic exposure was the strongest predictor (multivariate OR 5.1, p=0.009).
open_questions: External validation of SPIN score needed before clinical use
Does pre-chemo SRS timing improve pain response prospectively?

sites.intro: Neoadjuvant chemoRT revives a gastric survival benefit TOPGEAR couldn't show, though on a non-FLOT XELOX backbone.

studies.name: Neo-CRAG
studies.tldr: Adding neoadj chemoRT (45Gy/25fx) to periop XELOX improved mDFS 52.7 vs 24.4mo (HR 0.75) and mOS 67.5 vs 37.6mo (HR 0.78).
details: 🔍 Phase 3, 13 China centers, N=620 (310/arm), 2013-2022; high-risk cT3N2-3/cT4 gastric/EGJ adenoca, all D2-resected, 36.3% EGJ
🔍 RT: 45 Gy/25 fx concurrent, started after cycle 1, with reduced-dose chemo (oxaliplatin 100, cape 825) during RT
📊 Pathologic + locoregional gains, CRT vs CT (surgical cohort)
⚠️ Non-FLOT backbone (XELOX): RT benefit over modern perioperative FLOT SOC untested
🔗 Diverges from TOPGEAR (ph3), which found no survival benefit adding neoadj chemoRT to periop chemo
⚠️ G3+ hematologic toxicity higher with CRT: 14.6% (44/302) vs 10.3% (31/301)
📊 G3+ postoperative complications comparable: 9.0% (20/223) vs 7.6% (17/225)

verdict.rationale: Randomised ph3, primary DFS endpoint hit with OS benefit, so internally valid; but XELOX (non-FLOT) backbone and divergence from negative TOPGEAR keep RT's role contested.
verdict.audience: High-risk locally advanced gastric/EGJ adenoca, cT3N2-3 or cT4, D2-resectable

studies.significance: The RT-attributable read is locoregional control: LRR halved (9.4% vs 18.3% in R0 pts) alongside higher pCR and ypN0, a mechanistic chain (downstaging → fewer locoregional relapses → OS) that TOPGEAR's null never produced. 45Gy/25fx on a perioperative backbone, so it revives neoadjuvant RT for high-risk D2-resected LAGC, if you accept XELOX rather than FLOT.
open_questions: Does adding RT improve outcomes over a modern FLOT backbone?
Which subgroups (EGJ vs gastric, nodal burden) drive the RT benefit?
Generalizability beyond Chinese high-volume D2-resection centers?

Lay Summary
• In women having surgery to remove the breast for cancer treatment or prevention, the
optimal positioning of an implant (above the chest muscle, or below it) to rebuild the breast is
currently unclear.
• In the international PREPEC trial that was designed together with patient advocates, women
were assigned by chance (i.e., randomized) to receive the implant above or below the
muscle.
• The primary endpoint of the study was long-term patient-reported physical well-being of the
chest.
• Rebuilding the breast with an implant above the chest muscle resulted in better physical
well-being of the chest after two years (difference of 4.8 points on the BREAST-Q scale).
• However, implants placed above the chest muscle were more likely to undergo unexpected
removal or replacement (21% versus 15% of patients).

Perioperative MIBC: An Evolving Standard
Standard of care is defined by cisplatin-eligibility - with immunotherapy-based "sandwich" regimens
reshaping practice.
Cisplatin-ineligible or declining
• KEYNOTE-905: perioperative enfortumab vedotin + pembrolizumab (EVP) is now standard
• 3 cycles EVP → cystectomy → 6 cycles EV + 14 cycles P:
• NEW: VOLGA: preoperative EV + perioperative durvalumab + tremelimumab
• EV + durva improved EFS & OS vs cystectomy * approved adjuvant therapy; EV + durva/treme improved EFS (press release)?
Cisplatin-eligible
• NIAGARA: pre-op Gem/Cis + perioperative durvalumab (24-mo OS 82.2% vs 75.2% with Gem/Cis alone)
• VESPER: established ddMVAC > Gem/Cis on survival (5-yr OS 66% vs 57%) but greater toxicity*
• NEW: KEYNOTE-B15: perioperative EVP improved OS vs Gem/Cis (under FDA review)

Management Post-pCR: Continue or Stop?
For the sandwich regimens, standard practice continues planned
adjuvant therapy regardless of pathologic response.
Standard practice today
• NIAGARA regimen: Resume durvalumab for another 8 months post-cystectomy:
Perioperative EVP: Resume EVP post-cystectomy
Notably, not all patients in perioperative trials completed adjuvant therapy, sometimes due to toxicity
Cisplatin-based chemo alone: Surveillance after pCR is standard
Unanswered questions
What is the relative contribution of the pre- vs postoperative components?
Can adjuvant therapy be safely de-escalated based on pCR or other biomarkers?

Conclusions
• This single-arm study of amivantamab + lazertinib (n=49) in 1L atypical EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC
demonstrates a clinically meaningful median OS of ~3.5 years
• Responses were durable regardless of demographics, baseline tumor mutations, and disease characteristic:

with no new safety signals

in both common and atypical EGFR-mutated advanced NSCLC

Randomized, multicenter, open-label, phase 3 trial (NCT06448312)
Key Eligibility
• Locally advanced (stage IIIB/IIIC) or
metastatic (stage IV) NSCLC
• No prior systemic antitumor therapy
• No sensitizing EGFR or ALK
alteration

lab)
• ECOG score 0 or 1
Stratification factors
• Histology (squamous vs. non-squamous)
• PD-L1 TPS (1-49% vS. ≥ 50%)
• ECOG score (0 vs. 1)

image_ocr_texts:
The SPIN Score, a simple clinical score to predict pain response following celiac plexus
Abstract # 546246
Board # 194
radiosurgery for retroperitoneal cancer pain in pancreatic cancer.
Yaacov Lawrence Lawrence, Marcin Miszczyk, Dayssy Alexandra Diaz Pardo, Artur Aguiar, Dror Limon, M. Raphael Pfeffer, Michael Buckstein, Aisling S Barry, Adam P. Dicker, Camilla Zimmermann,
Jordan Kharofa, Ofer Margalit, Orly Yariv, Ofir Morag, David Hausner, Maoz Ben-Ayun, Talia Golan, Laura A. Dawson, Zvi Symon
SHEBA
Tel HaShomer
The Benjamin (Beni) Davidai
Department of
Radiation Oncology
Background
Celiac plexus radiosurgery is a novel non-invasive palliative treatment that was
recently added to NCCN guidelines. In the pivotal phase 2 trial (NCT03323489;
Lancet Oncol 2024;25:1070-79), pain response was 53% (95% Cl 42-64%).
Prespecified exploratory analysis identified age and BMI as predictors; however, no
clinically actionable patient selection tool was developed. We sought to identify
additional predictors and create a practical risk stratification score.
Methods
Post-hoc analysis of 90 evaluable patients from the phase 2 trial. Pain response was
defined per protocol (22-point reduction in average pain from baseline to three
weeks, on Brief Pain Inventory-Short Form). Candidate baseline predictors were
examined by univariate and multivariate logistic regression. Only variables
remaining significant in multivariate analysis were included in the final score to
ensure independence and avoid overfitting. Internal validation used bootstrap
resampling (500 iterations with replacement) to calculate optimism-corrected AUC.
Analysis performed using Stata IC/16.1.
Results
Univariate analysis identified 4 significant predictors: neurotoxic chemotherapy
exposure (OR 5.33, 95% Cl 2.13-13.4, p<0.001), baseline pain intensity (OR 1.73,
p=0.003), age (OR 1.06, p=0.014), and therapy line (OR 0.65, p=0.04). On
multivariate analysis, only neurotoxic exposure (OR 5.1, p=0.009) and baseline pain
(OR 1.8, p=0.003) remained independently significant predictors. Age and therapy
line lost significance due to collinearity.
Dose-response analysis
confirmed
pain threshold
optimization: >5
(61.5%
response), >6 (65.8%), >7 (83.3%), >8 (85.7%), with >6 providing optimal
discrimination.
We created the SPIN (Severe Pain + Intact Nerves) score (0-2
points): baseline Pain >6 (+1), No prior Neurotoxic chemotherapy (+1). Response
rates by score: 0 points: 32% (n=31); 1 point: 53% (n=40); 2 points: 89% (n=19). The
2-variable model achieved an apparent AUC=0.716. Following bootstrapping, the
optimism-corrected AUC was= 0.714
Conclusions
• The simple score allows identification of distinct patient subgroups with markedly different probabilities of pain response
following celiac plexus radiosurgery.
• This suggests the intervention should be considered earlier in the disease course, before exposure to neurotoxic agents.
External validation is needed prior to clinical implementation.
